channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18392] recovery-small test_160: FAIL: destroying OST objects are blocked 8
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/ae765827-d30b-42f3-bc6c-ee761d1177d1

test_160 failed with the following error:

== recovery-small test 160: MDT destroys are blocked by grouplocks ========================================================== 12:37:56 (1729687076)
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl set_param -n mdt.lustre-MDT0000.evict_client 5438529e-93f3-418c-80fe-28b863ed64ea
/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh: line 4072: /mnt/lustre/recon: Input/output error
CMD: onyx-119vm1.onyx.whamcloud.com,onyx-119vm2 /usr/bin/lfs df /mnt/lustre; uname -n >> /mnt/lustre/recon
Connected clients: onyx-119vm2.onyx.whamcloud.com
onyx-119vm1.onyx.whamcloud.com
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight
osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight=9
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight
osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight=9
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight
osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight=0
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight
osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight=0
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.destroys_in_flight
CMD: onyx-119vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.error_list
osp.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.error_list=
6 [0x240001b70:4812] llog=[0x23b:1] idx 10
6 [0x240001b70:4809] llog=[0x23b:1] idx 7
inflight 8
 recovery-small test_160: @@@@@@ FAIL: destroying OST objects are blocked 8

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4587 - 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_9.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4587 - 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
recovery-small test_160 - destroying OST objects are blocked 8
item.summary: recovery-small test_160: FAIL: destroying OST objects are blocked 8

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58096/
Subject: LU-18392 tests: watch destroys_in_flight recovery_small/160
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: a8182d531750a2ae81192ece810d70b4da430d8c

"Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58096
Subject: LU-18392 tests: watch destroys_in_flight recovery_small/160
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 495cbaceef65957586279370452bf2fc481abf34

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56846/
Subject: LU-18392 tests: hold group locks in recovery-small/160
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c110b20b806d5f8da561005780e277c035d98692
This test was added in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/55598 ("LU-15737 ofd: don't block destroys").

"Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56846
Subject: LU-18392 tests: hold group locks in recovery_small/160
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: d25ab895d470117d76092c846167beebf0cae262

OK, I think now I figured out what happened.
The new test case recovery_small/160 checks for destroys_in_flight, and it should be a low number to indicate the osp destroy on MDT doesn't get blocked by group locks.
The EIO looks scary on the OST/ofd, but that is not from ldiskfs, it was returned by ofd_destroy_by_fid() when the object is under group lock.
So when osp gets the EIO reply, the destroy_in_flight got reduced and the object is put on error_list to retry later after a timeout.

Now sometimes when osp retries the destroy, the rpc is actually done on ofd because the threads holding group lock is done, but it's not yet committed. On osp we got the reply with transid, but the commit callback still needs sometime to be triggered to reduce destroys_in_flight, when the test case checks te destoys_in_flight it will be high, as the rpcs are still pending, but eventually it will finish.

I think it's more like a test case issue, working on fixing the test case.
dongyang Would you please take a look?
The failure occurred 15 times in the past 6 months.
